note: Public summary file for Arun Verma personal website. Not raw clinical data.

variants.role: Gain-of-function IDH mutation defining the tumor biology.
variants.biology: IDH1 R132C changes tumor metabolism and is the reason IDH inhibition is central to the treatment story.
variants.treatment: Connects directly to IDH inhibitor therapy: ivosidenib first, then vorasidenib as the ongoing anchor.
variants.vaccine: Relevant to vaccine thinking because mutation-spanning peptides can be designed around patient-specific tumor mutations.

variants.role: Supports the astrocytoma molecular pattern with IDH mutation and TP53 alteration.
variants.vaf: 69.7% VAF reported by Tempus for ATRX G1712D
variants.biology: ATRX is involved in chromatin maintenance and telomere biology; ATRX loss is common in IDH-mutant astrocytoma.
variants.treatment: Not a simple direct drug target here, but important for classification and biological context.
variants.vaccine: Immune monitoring included an ATRX long peptide response signal in the CeGAT report.

variants.finding: Tumor TP53 alteration; germline discrepancy to reconcile
variants.role: Part of the astrocytoma-supportive molecular pattern, but source records differ on somatic versus germline interpretation.
variants.vaf: Tumor VAF values around 49-64% appear in reports depending on assay and notation
variants.biology: TP53 is a core genome-stability gene. In this story, the important design point is separating tumor-only change from inherited risk.
variants.treatment: Useful for diagnosis and risk framing; not a standalone treatment selector in this site version.
variants.vaccine: May contribute mutation-spanning peptide candidates if the specific tumor alteration is confirmed and represented in the vaccine design.

variants.finding: MGMT promoter status differs across records
variants.role: Some source records describe MGMT promoter methylation while the CeCurio vaccine report text says MGMT-promoter unmethylated.
variants.biology: MGMT promoter status matters in glioma treatment discussions, but this public page should show the discrepancy instead of flattening it into one claim.
variants.treatment: Before using MGMT for treatment interpretation, reconcile the source assay and report date.
variants.vaccine: Not a vaccine target; belongs in treatment-context biology.

variants.finding: Low TMB; MSI stable / MMR proficient
variants.role: Sets expectations for checkpoint-style immunotherapy biomarkers.
variants.vaf: TMB reported as low: 0.4-0.96 mut/Mb depending on report
variants.biology: A low mutation burden and MSI-stable profile generally means fewer classic checkpoint-response signals.
variants.treatment: This does not rule out a personalized vaccine strategy, but it argues against simplistic checkpoint eligibility narratives.
variants.vaccine: Personalized peptide vaccines can still be designed around selected mutation-spanning peptides even in low-TMB tumors.

variants.role: Triangle v2 flagged CDKN2A as a canonical tumor-type driver candidate, but this public page should treat it as context rather than a settled clinical conclusion.
variants.vaf: VAF not shown in the v2 card
variants.biology: CDKN2A/B status matters in IDH-mutant astrocytoma because homozygous deletion is associated with progression risk. The v2 report also noted no curated cancer-gene structural variant detected for CDKN2A/CDKN2B.
variants.treatment: Useful for risk framing and for understanding why copy-number and structural-variant review matters.
variants.vaccine: Not a central vaccine target in this public summary.

variants.role: These HLA alleles shape which tumor peptide fragments can plausibly be shown to T cells.
variants.biology: Class I HLA alleles in the v2 report were used for class-I neoantigen scoring. Class II alleles were present as helper context, but the run did not have NetMHCIIpan class-II scoring in cache.
variants.treatment: Important for understanding vaccine design and why HLA loss-of-heterozygosity would matter.
variants.vaccine: Top class-I candidates included HLA-B*35:01, HLA-C*03:04, HLA-C*04:01, and HLA-A*03:01 presentations.

variants.role: The v2 report ranked IDH1 R132C as the top SLP design candidate, pairing a short class-I epitope with a longer helper/flanked peptide.
variants.vaf: Tumor VAF 22.1%; CCF 88.4% in v2 report
variants.biology: IDH1 R132C is both the defining tumor mutation and a mutation-spanning peptide target in the vaccine design logic.
variants.treatment: Connects the targeted therapy story to the immune-training story: the same defining mutation is relevant to both.
variants.vaccine: Short epitope KPIIIGCHAY was paired with long peptide GWVKPIIIGCHAY; class-II binding score was not available in the cache.

variants.role: The v2 report ranked ATRX G1712D as a top SLP design candidate and the immune monitoring report included an ATRX long peptide response signal.
variants.biology: ATRX is part of the tumor identity and also appears in the vaccine-candidate layer.
variants.treatment: Not a direct drug target here, but a core part of the molecular story.
variants.vaccine: Short epitope LVDPDPDFV was paired with long peptide FNKALVDPDPDFV; class-II binding score was not available in the cache.

variants.role: This page should show what the run could and could not support.
variants.biology: Some signals are strong enough for storytelling; others need source files or orthogonal testing before stronger claims.
variants.treatment: HLA LOH was blocked because tumor and normal BAMs were missing, so it should not be used to filter vaccine candidates.
variants.vaccine: Class-II/long peptide rows exist, but NetMHCIIpan class-II scoring was absent in this cache.
